meta.description: Learning Hub publication generator and orchestrator. On a schedule (or on demand) it builds a premium staff security-awareness MAGAZINE (RSS digest -> AI editor -> A4 HTML -> Gotenberg PDF, written to /reports/learning-hub), then derives an e-learning COURSE strictly from that edition (Execute Workflow -> course generator) and registers a publication record (magazine + course, release date, 30-day window) in /reports/learning-hub/publications.json for the Learning Dashboard.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL || 'llama3.1:8b' }}",
  "stream": false,
  "keep_alive": "10m",
  "options": { "temperature": 0.4, "num_predict": 4500 },
  "messages": [ {"role":"system","content":"You are the editor of a monthly staff security-awareness magazine for a general (non-technical) workforce. Using the supplied news digest (and your own knowledge where the digest is thin), produce a SINGLE valid JSON object for this edition. Keys: title (a short, punchy edition title); weekOf (the edition label, e.g. a month and year); standfirst (ONE elegant cover sentence setting up the edition); intro (a 2-3 sentence editor's welcome); atAGlance (EXACTLY 3 items, each {value (a short striking figure drawn from the digest, e.g. \"68%\", \"$4.9M\", \"x3\" — or a 1-2 word fact if no number fits), label (what it means, max 12 words)}); feature (an object: headline, deck (one-line subtitle), body (4-5 short paragraphs separated by single newline characters), pullQuote (one quotable sentence lifted from or distilling the body), takeaways (3-4 short bullet strings), source (the publication name the feature draws on, or \"\"), url (the article link copied VERBATIM from the digest, or \"\")); threats (3-4 items, each {headline, summary (2-3 plain-English sentences), source (publication name or \"\"), url (link copied verbatim from the digest, or \"\")}); awareness (4-6 items, each {tip (a short imperative headline), detail (1-2 practical sentences)}); quals (3-4 items, each {name, provider, summary (1-2 sentences on who it suits), url (an official link if known, or \"\")}); glossary (3-5 items, each {term, definition (one plain-English sentence)}). For every url, only ever copy a link that actually appears in the supplied digest — never invent one; leave it \"\" if unsure. Keep everything engaging, concrete and free of jargon. Do NOT use markdown code fences. Output ONLY the JSON object."}, {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('Create this edition. Today is ' + ($json.date || '') + '. Base it on this news digest where useful:\n\n' + ($json.digest || '')) }} } ]
}
